name: dojo
context: Dojo - the universal knowledge layer for AI agents
info: Dojo creates a universal knowledge layer for AI agent skills and knowledge: discoverable, composable, hierarchical units of capability. Any agent, any framework, any LLM can query the registry and get back executable skills and knowledge with full context. This root explains what Dojo is, how to navigate the canonical `nodes/dojo` tree, and how the packaged [[dojo/skill]] turns the registry into a portable skill bundle for other agents.
triggers: where do i start in dojo
why does dojo link to manifest core
body:
# Dojo Overview

Dojo creates a universal knowledge layer for AI agent skills and knowledge, discoverable, composable, hierarchical units of capability. Any agent, any framework, any LLM can query the registry and get back executable skills and knowledge with full context.

The knowledge layer is what makes Dojo more than npm. Every node in the tree eco, standard, skill, context, sub can carry rich, linked, wiki-like content. They are the Obsidian notes of the agent world. Context nodes are how agents learn.

Dojo is a knowledge graph with executable skills. The spec starts from hierarchical capability units, but the important extension is the knowledge layer: nodes can carry `context`, `info`, `body`, `sections`, `aliases`, `links`, `related`, and scripts. That lets an agent discover, learn, and execute from the same graph instead of jumping between disconnected docs and tools.

At the base level, Dojo defines the manifest in `node.json`. At runtime, it becomes a searchable graph spanning five node types: `ecosystem`, `standard`, `skill`, `context`, and `sub`. The spec explicitly treats `context` nodes as first-class citizens, which is why Dojo is better understood as a knowledge-native registry than as a package catalog with docs bolted on.

## Design

1. **Hierarchical** — Nodes form trees, ecosystem → standard → skill → sub-skill
2. **Self-describing** — Every node carries its own context, docs, and schema
3. **Composable** — Skills reference and depend on other skills
4. **Agent-native** — Designed for LLM agents to discover and execute, not just humans
5. **Wiki-like** — Rich info fields, linked references, living documentation
6. **Executable** — Scripts are first-class: agents run them, not just read them

Five node types. The nesting is **flexible** — any type can appear at any depth, with a few constraints.

```
ecosystem (root)
│   Broad domain. No parent. The top of the tree.
│   Examples: openai, github, docker, aws, postgres
│
├── standard
│   A protocol, spec, or convention. Can live under an eco,
│   a skill, or even another standard.
│   Examples: rest, graphql, oauth2, openapi
│
├── skill
│   A concrete, executable capability with scripts.
│   Can live under an eco, a standard, or another skill.
│   Examples: query, deploy, send, build
│
├── context
│   An info-only node. Wiki-like documentation with no scripts.
│   Used for reference material, guides, specs, or grouping.
│   Can live anywhere in the tree.
│   Examples: indexing-guide, caching-strategies, troubleshooting
│
└── sub
    A specialized variant of a skill.
    Must have a skill parent.
    Examples: deploy-preview, query-explain, build-multi-stage
```

```
openai                                eco
├── chat                              skill
│   ├── complete                      sub
│   └── stream                        sub
├── embeddings                        skill
├── assistants                        skill
│   └── threads                       skill (skill under skill)
│       ├── create                    sub
│       └── run                       sub
├── models                            context (info about available models)
└── rate-limits                       context (guide to rate limit handling)
```

## Why dojo

Dojo is the Google, NPM, and wiki for AI capabilities. It is a knowledge layer and simple source for any knowledge and skills an agent might need. It is designed to be the canonical source of truth for agent-facing capabilities, with a standard format that any framework can implement and any agent can query.

Agents need a source for not just what but how. As agents proliferate, they need a way to discover what they can do and how to do it. Dojo provides a universal layer for skills and knowledge: discoverable, composable, hierarchical units of capability.

## [github](https://github.com/theslashdojo) | [discord](https://discord.gg/ANA4cGV9D7) | [spec](https://github.com/theslashdojo/dojo/blob/main/SPEC.md) | [twitter](https://x.com/not_pr0)

```
npm i slashdojo
#Install this skill to your agent's skill directory
dojo bundle dojo/dojoskill ~/.codex/skills
dojo bundle dojo/dojoskill ~/.claude/skills/
```

sections.title: How to use Dojo
sections.body: Use the tree by goal. If you are new, start at [[dojo/sdk]] and then [[dojo/knowledge#body-and-sections]]. If you are authoring, move to [[dojo/contributing#quickstart]] and then [[dojo/validate#when-to-run]]. If you are integrating a client, read [[dojo/api#discovery-routes]], execute [[dojo/api/query#knowledge-workflow]], and fetch [[dojo/skill#bundle-workflow]] if another agent needs the portable package. If you are debugging runtime behavior, open [[dojo/architecture#source-of-truth]]. If you are releasing changes, finish with [[dojo/publish#release-flow]].

sections.title: Spec
sections.body: The source specification lives in `SPEC.md` and is summarized in [[dojo/spec]]. Read section 1 for the high-level overview, section 2 for the `node.json` contract, section 3 for type hierarchy and parent rules, and section 4 for the knowledge layer. The `dojo/spec` node turns those ideas into addressable sections that the rest of this tree can link to precisely.

sections.title: Knowledge graph
sections.body: The knowledge graph is the runtime instantiation of the spec's contract. The `nodes/dojo` tree is the source of truth for the graph at runtime, and every node in that tree is a graph node with links, sections, body text, and context. The graph is what agents query and execute from, and it is what the API serves up.

sections.title: Workflow map
Next step by goal
sections.body: Open [[dojo/contributing#quickstart]] if you need to create or edit a node. Open [[dojo/knowledge#authoring-patterns]] if the content feels thin or hard to search. Open [[dojo/api#knowledge-routes]] if you need the read-side HTTP surface. Open [[dojo/skill#bundle-workflow]] if you need the packaged skill for another agent runtime. Run [[dojo/validate#what-gets-checked]] if you are reviewing quality. Use [[dojo/glossary#reading-path]] if the vocabulary around sections, backlinks, or learn flows is slowing you down.
